title: NHS England Updates Rules to Improve Patient Communication
subtitle: New standards aim to provide better service for millions waiting for hospital care

summary.en: NHS England is introducing new rules to improve how hospitals communicate with patients waiting for care. Patients will now receive at least three weeks' notice before their appointments or operations. This change follows reports that many people were not receiving information in time. The NHS hopes to provide better service by using methods similar to modern online shopping companies. These new standards aim to reduce patient anxiety and improve overall satisfaction with the health service.
summary.zh: 英國國家醫療保健服務正在推行新規定，以改善醫院與候診病患之間的溝通。病患現在將在預約或手術前至少收到三週的通知。此項變革源於先前有報導指出許多民眾未能及時收到資訊。該機構希望透過仿效現代網路購物企業的方法來提供更好的服務。這些新標準旨在減輕病患焦慮，並提升大眾對醫療服務的整體滿意度。

paragraphs.en: NHS England has announced a major update to how hospitals communicate with patients who are waiting for medical care. Under new rules, hospitals must now give patients at least three weeks' notice before an operation, a diagnostic test, or a meeting with a consultant. This change is part of a broader plan to improve the experience of patients and ensure they are no longer left in the dark about their health status.
paragraphs.zh: 英國國家醫療保健服務（NHS England）宣布了一項重大更新，旨在改善醫院與候診病患之間的溝通方式。根據新規定，醫院現在必須在手術、診斷測試或專科醫師會診前，至少提前三週通知病患。這項變革是改善病患就醫體驗的廣泛計畫之一，確保他們不再對自己的健康狀況一無所知。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian Health, this move comes after research by the King’s Fund revealed that nearly one in four patients were being notified about their appointments only after the scheduled time had already passed. For many people, this lack of information has caused significant anxiety and frustration. Patients have frequently reported feeling forgotten while waiting for care, sometimes even unsure if their initial referral from a general practitioner had been accepted by the hospital.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報健康版》（The Guardian Health）報導，此舉是在國王基金會（King’s Fund）的研究顯示近四分之一的病患在預約時間過後才收到通知後所採取的行動。對許多人來說，資訊的匱乏造成了極大的焦慮與挫折感。病患經常反映在等待治療期間感到被遺忘，有時甚至不確定家庭醫師的轉診是否已被醫院受理。

paragraphs.en: Jim Mackey, the chief executive of NHS England, described the current situation as "clearly unacceptable." He noted that the health service must fix the basics of how it treats people, even before they arrive for their actual treatment. To address these issues, the NHS is looking to the customer service models used by successful online businesses. By adopting these practices, the organization hopes to provide what Mackey calls "five-star customer service" to the millions of people currently on waiting lists.
paragraphs.zh: 英國國家醫療保健服務執行長麥基（Jim Mackey）形容目前的情況「顯然無法接受」。他指出，醫療服務體系必須在病患抵達接受治療前，就先改善對待民眾的基本方式。為了處理這些問題，該機構正參考成功網路企業的客戶服務模式。透過採用這些做法，該組織希望為目前數百萬名在等待名單上的民眾，提供麥基所稱的「五星級客戶服務」。

paragraphs.en: There are currently about 6 million people in England waiting for approximately 7 million different tests, operations, and appointments. The new "patient experience standards" aim to ensure that these individuals receive clear and timely updates. In the future, patients will be able to use the NHS app to see when a hospital specialist has accepted their referral and receive confirmation that they are officially on the waiting list. For those who prefer traditional methods, hospitals will continue to provide updates through letters or text messages.
paragraphs.zh: 目前英格蘭約有六百萬人正在等待約七百萬項不同的檢查、手術與門診預約。新的「病患體驗標準」旨在確保這些民眾能收到清晰且即時的更新。未來，病患將能透過英國國家醫療保健服務應用程式（NHS app）查看醫院專科醫師是否已受理其轉診，並確認他們已正式列入等待名單。對於偏好傳統方式的民眾，醫院將持續透過信件或簡訊提供更新。

paragraphs.en: William Pett, the interim director of policy and external affairs at Healthwatch England, a group that advocates for patients, welcomed the announcement. He stated that the new standards send a clear signal that high-quality communication is just as important to patients as the actual length of their wait. Pett explained that for too long, patients have dealt with missing letters and delayed information, which often made them feel like they were not a priority for the health system.
paragraphs.zh: 為病患權益倡議的團體「英國健康觀察」（Healthwatch England）政策與對外事務臨時總監佩特（William Pett）對此宣布表示歡迎。他指出，新標準傳達了一個明確的訊號，即高品質的溝通對病患而言，與實際等待時間的長短同樣重要。佩特解釋說，長期以來，病患一直面臨信件遺失與資訊延遲的問題，這常讓他們覺得自己並非醫療體系優先考量的對象。

paragraphs.en: This update builds upon previous efforts to modernize the NHS. With at least 40 million people already using the NHS app, the organization is well-positioned to use digital tools to keep patients informed. The goal is to move away from a system where patients must ask multiple times for basic information and instead create a more transparent process. By providing consistent updates, the NHS hopes to reduce the confusion that has historically made navigating the health service feel difficult for many families.
paragraphs.zh: 此次更新建立在先前推動醫療體系現代化的努力之上。隨著至少四千萬人已在使用該應用程式，該組織已準備好利用數位工具讓病患隨時掌握資訊。目標是擺脫病患必須多次詢問基本資訊的體系，轉而建立一個更透明的流程。透過提供一致的更新，該機構希望減少過去讓許多家庭在醫療服務中感到難以適應的混亂情況。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the success of these new rules will depend on how quickly England’s 205 NHS trusts can change their internal communication systems. Mackey has instructed hospitals to implement these standards as soon as possible. While the length of waiting lists remains a significant challenge for the health service, these communication improvements are intended to make the waiting process much easier for patients to manage. By treating patients more like customers who deserve clear and timely information, the NHS aims to restore trust and improve the overall quality of care across the country.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，這些新規則的成功將取決於英格蘭兩百零五個醫療信託機構（NHS trusts）調整內部溝通系統的速度。麥基已指示醫院盡快落實這些標準。儘管等待名單的長度仍是醫療服務的一大挑戰，但這些溝通上的改進旨在讓病患更容易管理等待過程。透過將病患視為值得獲得清晰且即時資訊的客戶，該機構目標在於重建信任，並提升全國整體的醫療品質。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be kept uninformed about something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 被蒙在鼓裡，對某事一無所知。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思是「留在黑暗中」，這裡指病人對自己的健康狀況缺乏資訊。文中：This change is part of a broader plan to improve the experience of patients and ensure they are no longer left in the dark about their health status.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To repair or improve something that is not working correctly.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「固定」或「修理物品」，這裡指改善服務流程。文中：He noted that the health service must fix the basics of how it treats people, even before they arrive for their actual treatment.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To use previous achievements or work as a base for further development.
keyTerms.noteZh: 指在既有的基礎上進行改進或擴展。文中：This update builds upon previous efforts to modernize the NHS.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: People awaiting hospital treatment to get three weeks’ notice under NHS England plans

previousArticles.title: NHS England Introduces New Rules to Improve Patient Communication
previousArticles.summaryEn: NHS England is introducing new rules to improve how hospitals communicate with patients. Patients will now receive at least three weeks' notice before their hospital appointments or operations. This change follows reports that many people were not receiving information until after their appointment dates had passed. The health service is looking to modern businesses for inspiration on how to provide better customer service. These updates will be managed through the NHS app, as well as by letter or text for those who prefer it.

quiz.question: According to the new NHS rules, what is the minimum advance notice hospitals must provide to patients for medical appointments?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第一段明確指出：「hospitals must now give patients at least three weeks' notice before an operation, a diagnostic test, or a meeting with a consultant」。選項 A、C、D 皆與文中規定的時間不符，屬於對具體細節的錯誤記憶。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why might a patient have felt 'forgotten' by the NHS before these new rules were introduced?

options.A: Because the NHS app was not available to the public.
options.B: Because they were not allowed to contact their general practitioner.
options.C: Because they received notification of appointments only after the scheduled time had passed.
options.D: Because hospitals were not allowed to send letters or text messages.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第二段，研究指出近四分之一的病人是在預約時間過後才收到通知，這種缺乏資訊的情況導致病人感到被遺忘與焦慮。選項 A 與文章第六段提到的 4000 萬用戶矛盾；選項 B 文中未提及；選項 D 則與第四段提到的傳統通知方式不符。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'the health service must fix the basics of how it treats people', what does the word 'basics' most likely refer to?

options.A: The fundamental principles or essential requirements of service.
options.B: The lowest level of medical training for staff.
options.C: The initial physical examinations performed by nurses.
options.D: The basic equipment needed for surgery.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境下，「fix the basics」指的是改善服務中核心且必要的溝通流程，而非指醫療技術或設備。選項 B、C、D 皆過度聚焦於醫療技術面，偏離了文章討論「客戶服務」與「溝通管理」的主題。

quiz.question: What is the primary goal of the NHS's new 'patient experience standards'?

options.A: To reduce the number of people on the waiting list.
options.B: To replace all traditional letters with digital app notifications.
options.C: To force hospitals to hire more consultants.
options.D: To improve communication and transparency for patients awaiting care.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。全文的核心在於介紹 NHS 如何透過新的溝通標準，讓等待中的病人獲得即時資訊，減少焦慮並提升透明度。選項 A 是挑戰而非溝通目標；選項 B 忽略了第四段提到仍保留傳統方式；選項 C 文中並未提及增加人力是主要手段。
